title: India now has 190 nuclear warheads: What's driving New Delhi's atomic buildup?
content: India's nuclear warheads have increased to 190. This expansion is part of a modernization effort. Global nuclear powers are upgrading their arsenals. Asia faces rising tensions and strategic competition. India's nuclear strategy focuses on China and Pakistan. New delivery systems and sea-based deterrents are being developed. The global arms control framework is weakening.

title: US judge strikes down Donald Trump's $100k visa fee, calls it unlawful
content: A US federal judge has ruled against a $100,000 fee imposed by former President Donald Trump on new H-1B visas. The judge found the fee unlawful. This fee significantly increased the cost for highly skilled foreign workers seeking to enter the United States. The ruling came after a lawsuit filed by state attorneys general.

title: India coach 'doesn't know' if Rohit and Hardik have cleared fitness tests
content: Hardik has been recovering from a back spasm, while Rohit sustained a hamstring injury during the IPL. Both players missed multiple matches during the tournament.

title: US warplane strikes, disables oil tanker near Oman; crew of 24 Indians evacuated
content: An unladen oil tanker, MT Marivex, carrying 24 Indian crew members, was disabled and caught fire in the Gulf of Oman following a US military action. The US stated an F/A-18 Super Hornet fired a precision munition after the vessel allegedly violated a blockade of Iran's ports and ignored directions. All Indian seafarers were safely evacuated with Omani assistance.

title: Google AI CEO Demis Hassabis on AGI: Humans only have a few years left to prepare
content: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis warns humanity is on the cusp of Artificial General Intelligence, potentially within four years, urging immediate preparation. He views current AI agents as a societal stress test and highlights the risks of recursive self-improvement. Hassabis also criticized tech companies for laying off engineers, advocating for increased productivity instead.

title: 'Almost got into a fight’: Donald Trump-Netanyahu tensions flare over Lebanon
content: US President Trump reportedly clashed with Israeli PM Netanyahu over recent military actions in Lebanon and Iran. A senior US envoy suggested a near confrontation, with Trump urging restraint to avoid derailing diplomatic efforts with Iran. Trump publicly called for an immediate ceasefire, emphasizing his role in brokering a deal.

title: Nightclub controversy! England captain Ben Stokes faces ECB probe
content: England's preparations for the second Test against New Zealand are marred by controversy as captain Ben Stokes and all-rounder Gus Atkinson face an investigation for an alleged breach of team protocols. The ECB confirmed an incident occurred at a nightclub early Monday morning, with further details pending the investigation's completion.

title: Not in room, yet in conversation: CJP crawls into INDIA bloc huddle
content: Opposition leaders at the INDIA bloc meeting discussed the emerging youth-led Cockroach Janata Party (CJP). While some expressed concerns about its intent, others, including Omar Abdullah and Mamata Banerjee, supported engaging with the movement, viewing it as a sign of genuine youth dissent and a healthy space for civil movements.

title: As Jamie Dimon calls Elon Musk 'Edison of our time', Musk says: There will not be much AC left
content: As SpaceX eyes a historic IPO, JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon likened Elon Musk to Thomas Edison. This sparked online debate, with many championing Nikola Tesla. Musk himself weighed in, acknowledging Edison's brilliance while asserting DC's future dominance over AC, citing solar, batteries, and EVs as key drivers.

title: Allies 'large heart' message to Cong, Mamata's new tone, DMK shadow: Inside INDIA bloc meet
content: INDIA bloc allies voiced concerns to Congress during their first post-election meeting. The CPM criticized remarks made during the Kerala polls, while others urged a more accommodating approach. Leaders called for the Congress to be 'large-hearted' towards regional partners, emphasizing unity against the BJP. Rahul Gandhi assured commitment to the alliance.

title: Several Indian workers killed in Dubai after minibus hits truck
content: Tragedy struck in Dubai as a minibus carrying Indian workers collided with a truck, resulting in multiple fatalities and injuries. Authorities revealed the truck had stopped unexpectedly due to a technical issue. Preliminary investigations suggest the bus driver's inattentive driving and failure to maintain a safe distance contributed to the devastating crash.

title: US largest private-sector employer’s message to 2mn-plus staff on AI replacing their jobs
content: Walmart assures its 2.1 million employees that AI will enhance their roles, not replace them. Company leaders emphasized that while technology will be integral to future operations, human employees will remain central. AI is already being implemented to improve efficiency, such as a tool helping truck drivers optimize loads and reduce empty miles.

title: Another mutiny brewing? TMC turmoil reaches Parliament - what we know so far
content: Trinamool Congress is experiencing a significant political shift. Several party MPs have reportedly written to the Lok Sabha Speaker expressing their support for the BJP-led NDA. This follows recent internal party challenges and a poor performance in the West Bengal elections. The move aims to align with the NDA's political course.

title: ISL clubs table four-year plan, offer AIFF Rs 15.4 crore after sports minister talks
content: Sports minister Mansukh Mandaviya assured ISL clubs that the 2026-27 season will proceed as scheduled after a key meeting involving clubs, AIFF officials and ex-chief Praful Patel. A new proposal favouring a club-led model, under which AIFF would receive Rs 15.4 crore — higher than the projected earnings from the proposed Genius Sports partnership. Stadium costs also emerged as a topic of concern.

title: Modern-day Brahmastra? How hypersonic missiles could change the rules of war
content: India is rapidly advancing in hypersonic weapon technology, achieving a significant scramjet breakthrough. This development positions the nation among global leaders like the US, China, and Russia, who are all racing to develop these near-unstoppable weapons. Hypersonic systems, flying above Mach 5, pose a new era of warfare, demanding advanced defense strategies.

title: Patrick & Brittany Mahomes net worth 2026: Wealth, earnings & luxury life
content: Patrick Mahomes is embracing his quick recovery by enjoying sports with wife Brittany Mahomes. Their shared passion for sports extends to investments in various franchises. They co-own the Kansas City Current. The couple has been high school sweethearts. They have built a life together with three children and a luxurious Missouri mansion.

title: Why Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are linked to a MSG wedding rumor
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are reportedly set to tie the knot at New York City’s Madison Square Garden on July 3, 2026. The choice is driven by unparalleled privacy. With plans for underground access and street closures to keep paparazzi at bay. The iconic venue also holds personal significance for Swift. She has performed multiple times there. As for Kelce’s connection, he was recently spotted for a Knicks vs. Cavaliers game at MSG with Swift.

title: Dylan Larkin’s 3-team wishlist raises pressure on Red Wings
content: Dylan Larkin’s reported three-team trade wishlist includes the Florida Panthers, Minnesota Wild, and Vegas Golden Knights. The Detroit Red Wings captain controls the market with his no-trade clause, but Steve Yzerman still needs a serious return. Here is why Larkin’s list creates a messy offseason problem for Detroit and every contender involved.

title: Visakhapatnam tragedy: 8 burnt to death as molten metal spills on workers at steel plant
content: A tragic accident at Vizag Steel Plant claimed eight lives and injured six workers. A hot metal ladle fell, causing a massive fire in Steel Melting Shop One. Bodies are difficult to identify due to severe burns. Injured workers are receiving treatment for extensive burns. This incident is the plant's worst since a 2012 explosion.

title: Good news for fans: Earlier start times for Sooryavanshi's likely debut series
content: India will play five T20Is and three ODIs in England between July 1 and July 19. According to a report by ESPNcricinfo, the ECB has advanced the start times of three evening T20Is by one hour following discussions with broadcasters. The matches were originally scheduled to begin at 6.30 pm local time (11 pm IST), as announced last year. However, they will now start at 5.30 pm local time (10 pm IST), making them more viewer-friendly for Indian audiences.

title: The INDIA bloc divide: Why Mamata is all in but Stalin is walking 'ekla chalo' path
content: Both lost power. Both saw their political dominance challenged. Yet while Mamata Banerjee has turned to the INDIA bloc as a source of relevance and protection, MK Stalin has chosen to focus on rebuilding his own turf. Their contrasting responses offer a glimpse into the next phase of opposition politics in India.

title: Trump introduced her to Epstein. Then she dated Bill Gates...
content: The story matters because Walker appears to have been both a beneficiary of Epstein’s patronage and one of the people who helped him gain access to Gates’s circle after his 2008 conviction for soliciting a minor for prostitution.

title: Charity begins at home: Hungary lawmakers vote unanimously to cut own salaries by 40%
content: Hungary's parliament has unanimously slashed lawmakers' salaries by 40%, a move championed by Prime Minister Peter Magyar to curb spending and mend public finances. This significant pay cut, effective next month, also impacts other high-ranking officials and allowances, fulfilling a key reform promise. The government aims to stabilize its economy and secure EU funds.

title: 'Meetings may soon be held in a car': BJP mocks shrinking INDIA bloc
content: The Bharatiya Janata Party has criticized the INDIA bloc's recent meeting in Delhi. BJP MP Sambit Patra stated the opposition alliance has lost public support and credibility. He mocked the shrinking scale of their gatherings. INDIA bloc leaders discussed issues like the NEET-CBSE row and the economy. They plan to meet every two months.

title: Alexander Zverev’s French Open win shows that SRK was right in Om Shanti Om
content: In his prime, Alexander conquered much of the landmass known to ancient Greeks, which was still only 3-4% of the actual landmass of the world, and supposedly wept that there was no land left to conquer. His namesake, on the other hand, had another reason to weep: his inability to conquer the one territory that had kept defeating him, a Grand Slam.

title: No future tariffs, fair rates, end 'forced labour' probe: What India wants from US trade deal
content: India is pushing for preferential tariffs and guarantees against future hikes in ongoing trade talks with the US. New Delhi seeks clarity on proposed US tariffs linked to forced labor and excess industrial capacity concerns. Both nations aim to finalize an interim trade agreement, with discussions gaining momentum after recent high-level meetings.

title: Nana Patekar reveals why he chose village life over Bollywood glamour
content: Veteran actor Nana Patekar's candid conversation on 'Kaun Banega Crorepati 16' about his return to village life is captivating audiences. He explained his detachment from the film industry's glamour, stating, "I come from a village, and I will always remain a village man." Patekar emphasized that limited needs simplify life, finding solace amidst mountains rather than city walls. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Gallantry awards: Prez Murmu honours 51 winners; Gaganyaan astronaut among recipients
content: President Murmu honored 51 personnel with Kirti, Vir, and Shaurya Chakras for exceptional bravery and sacrifice. The awards recognized acts of raw courage across various operational theatres, including elite forces and CAPFs. Notably, Gaganyaan astronaut-designate Group Captain Prasanth Balakrishnan Nair received a Kirti Chakra for his distinguished service.

title: LIC Housing Finance asked to pay Rs 10 lakh after losing borrower’s original property papers
content: LIC Housing Finance must pay Rs 10 lakh compensation for losing a borrower's original property documents after loan repayment. The consumer commission upheld the order, finding the company guilty of deficiency in service. Despite the borrower's repeated requests, the lender failed to return the crucial title deeds, leading to the significant penalty.

title: 'Does he really want to?': De Villiers has one big 'doubt' about Sooryavanshi
content: Speaking at an event in Mumbai on Sunday, de Villiers said Sooryavanshi has the skill to succeed in Test cricket but questioned whether the teenager would want to take that path.

title: SpaceX IPO explained: Can Indians invest and how does it work?
content: SpaceX's monumental $1.75 trillion IPO is generating immense global interest, with bankers reporting double the demand for available shares. While Indian investors cannot directly participate in the IPO allotment, they can invest in SpaceX shares on secondary markets after listing through international brokers under the RBI's LRS scheme.

title: TMC revolt knocks Parliament: 20 MPs seek tie-up with NDA as Mamata attends INDIA meet
content: At least 20 TMC MPs have written to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla expressing their support for the BJP-led NDA, following Rajya Sabha MP Sukhendu Shekhar Ray's resignation. Rebel leader Ritabrata Banerjee cited disillusionment with the party's leadership and parliamentary treatment as reasons for the growing dissent.

title: Manav Suthar joins Rohit and Shreyas, becomes only the 9th Indian to ...
content: Manav Suthar made a dream Test debut for India. He earned Player of the Match for his outstanding bowling performance. Suthar took seven wickets in the match. India secured a massive innings victory over Afghanistan. His debut effort is among the best by an Indian bowler. Suthar emphasized consistency and patience in Test cricket.

title: Chosen one Manav Suthar gives India hope with dip, revs and perfect 'tappa'
content: Manav Suthar on Sunday bagged a six-wicket haul in his debut Test outing. The 23-year-old, who was given the nod ahead of Harsh Dubey for the one-off Test against Afghanistan, became only the tenth Indian bowler and seventh Indian spinner to claim a five-wicket haul on Test debut.

title: INDIA bloc meeting concludes, consensus on 5 issues including Pradhan's resignation
content: INDIA bloc leaders met, agreeing to petition the Chief Justice on electoral integrity and demanding the Education Minister's resignation over exam controversies. They also called for an all-party meeting on the economy and pledged to meet bi-monthly. Parliament coordination will continue daily during the monsoon session.

title: NFL trade rumors: Cowboys eye $20M raiders defensive upgrade
content: The Dallas Cowboys are once again being linked to Raiders star Maxx Crosby after reportedly trying to trade for him earlier this offseason. ESPN insider Dan Graziano believes a midseason deal remains possible if Las Vegas struggles. Jerry Jones is still viewed as a potential suitor, though any move would require major draft assets and significant salary cap planning for Dallas.

title: ‘Matter of utmost concern’: India urges de-escalation as Israel-Iran exchange fire
content: India has voiced grave concern over escalating Middle East violence, urging immediate de-escalation and diplomatic talks. The ongoing conflict, now over 100 days, is causing immense human suffering and impacting the global economy. Indian missions in Israel and Iran have issued urgent travel and safety advisories for citizens, emphasizing caution and immediate departure where necessary.

title: Bajaj Allianz wins mediclaim dispute after probe finds hospital records manipulated
content: A Delhi consumer court overturned a reimbursement order for a mediclaim, stating an insurer was justified in rejecting a claim. Investigations revealed significant flaws and discrepancies in hospital records, including alleged non-cooperation from the insured. The court emphasized the importance of considering investigator reports, concluding the insurer acted within policy terms.

title: Fitness at what cost? Cardiac emergencies, fatal mishaps put gym safety in spotlight
content: Recent tragic incidents in Indian gyms, including celebrity deaths from cardiac arrest and fatal accidents, highlight critical safety concerns. These events underscore the urgent need for mandatory health screenings, proper supervision, and adherence to safety standards within fitness centers to prevent further avoidable tragedies.

title: Rs 13,000 cr airport at Great Nicobar Island to improve India's forward posture in Indian Ocean
content: India is investing Rs 13,000 crore in Great Nicobar Island for a dual-use airport, bolstering civilian connectivity and military readiness near the vital Strait of Malacca. This strategic project enhances surveillance of crucial shipping lanes, impacting China's energy security and strengthening India's Indo-Pacific influence. The development also includes a trans-shipment hub, aiming to boost India's economic standing.

title: 24 Indian sailors rescued from ship hit by U.S. missile
content: Marivex sanctioned by the U.S. last December, had earlier carried Iranian oil to India; U.S. Central Command says the tanker violated the ongoing blockade by attempting to sail to an Iranian port; sailors likely to reach India in two days

title: INDIA bloc in disarray, lacks ground presence: BJP
content: Its constituent parties are grappling with electoral losses and internal conflict while the BJP stays focused on welfare initiatives and measures to make India a developed country, says Sambit Patra

title: BGB delegation arrives in India for border talks with BSF
content: The talks are taking place against the backdrop of tensions between the two border-guarding forces over allegations by Bangladesh that the BSF has been sending undocumented Bangladeshis across the border through multiple land entry points.

title: 7 Indian workers killed, 5 critically injured in road accident in Dubai
content: The accident resulted in seven fatalities and nine injuries, including five serious and four moderate injuries, the Dubai Police said, while adding that all injured were transported to the hospital for treatment

title: Akhilesh spreading ‘fabricated lies’ about Ram temple, says BJP
content: Temple trust says “nothing noteworthy has come to light yet” in the audit; Akhilesh Yadav calls response “suspicious”; BJP says “narrative of lies will never succeed” and SP will lose 2027 polls

title: Strategic, all-weather Zojila Tunnel between Kashmir and Kargil to see final breakthrough
content: Road Transport and Highways Minister Nitin Gadkari to oversee the final blasting from the Kargil side on June 9; the 13.14 km-long tunnel cuts through the mighty Himalayas at an altitude of 11,578 feet and connects the Kashmir valley with Ladakh’s Kargil

title: Solar firms move Karnataka HC over “unreasonable” domestic-cell mandate
content: The writ petition challenges Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) orders that enforce ALMM List-II — the approved list of domestic solar-cell manufacturers — for projects commissioned on or after June 1, 2026

title: Major IAS reshuffle in Kerala; B. Ashok is Principal Secretary, Higher Education, and N. Prashanth, Special Secretary, Sports
content: Rathan U. Kelkar will hold full additional charge as Secretary to the newly-formed Department for the Welfare of the Elderly as well as the departments of Coastal Shipping and Inland Navigation, and Information and Public Relations

title: Analysis: INDIA bloc closes ranks around Congress, but not without caveats
content: With defeat of Trinamool Congress in West Bengal and exodus of MPs & MLAs from its ranks, Congress has no challenger within the coalition. Trinamool has also indicated a possible alliance with Congress in 2029 general elections.

title: India expands nuclear arsenal to around 190 warheads, says SIPRI Yearbook 2026
content: SIPRI notes New Delhi’s ongoing nuclear modernisation programme is increasingly focused on developing longer-range weapons capable of reaching targets across China

title: Government defends Great Nicobar development plans, cites strategic and environmental safeguards
content: Elaborating about the project, official says the project is expected to transform Great Nicobar into a key maritime and economic hub while enhancing India’s defence capabilities in the strategically significant Indian Ocean Region

title: RS polls: BJP springs a surprise by choosing OBC academician Nagaraja as candidate
content: With the BJP selecting M. Nagaraja, the speculation over former Prime Minister Deve Gowda getting one more opportunity ends

title: Karnataka High Court upholds law allowing BWSSB to lay sewer through private properties without acquisition of land
content: Pointing out that the provisions of the Act requires the BWSSB to cause as little damage as possible and mandates full compensation for damage caused, the court says that ‘the statutory scheme, therefore, does not contemplate unrestricted occupation or appropriation of land...’

title: No incriminating material found in data retrieved from TNPDCL’s  stolen hard disks
content: While 18 hard disks were reported stolen, the police found 34 hard disks in the possession of the suspect in Bengaluru. The case has been transferred to CB-CID from the Greater Chennai Police

title: Sigma Advanced bags $21.97 million artillery shell body export order
content: The shell body forms the primary structural element of a 155 mm artillery projectile, the company said, adding the order is for a customer in North America

title: Heavy rain to continue in Kerala; red alert for three districts on June 9
content: Holiday declared for educational institutions in Kasaragod, Kannur, and Kozhikode districts on June 9

title: City girl headed for Maths Olympiad in Shanghai
content: 17-year-old Sanjana Philo Chacko has been selected to the six-member Indian team for the International Mathematical Olympiad at Shanghai next month

title: SFI alleges ‘saffronisation’ of higher education, protests at MG University
content: Over the appointment of the interim Vice-Chancellor at the Mahatma Gandhi University and nominations of members to its senate by the Governor

title: Sukhendu Sekhar Roy resigns from Rajya Sabha, and Trinamool Congress
content: The resignation brings to the fore differences among Trinamool MPs; speculation is rife that more MPs may follow suit

title: Watch: INDIA bloc’s latest huddle: What emerged behind closed doors? | Above the Fold | 08.06.2026
content: From the INDIA bloc’s latest strategy meeting and growing alliance tensions to the escalating U.S.-Iran conflict, a major arrest in West Bengal and Xi Jinping’s North Korea visit — The top stories of the day.

title: Visakhapatnam Steel Plant accident: eight dead in ladle blast, fire at SMS-1
content: A ladle of liquid steel explodes at the Continuous Casting Department, sparking a fire and spilling molten steel across the shop floor at the RINL-run plant

title: Minimum wage revision brings little relief to workers
content: Despite a long-awaited revision after two decades, Telangana’s new minimum wages offer little relief, with many workers seeing negligible gains, lower real earnings and rates trailing Central government benchmarks

title: Digital fertiliser reform faces first test in kharif season
content: Government says APAIMS 2.0 will streamline distribution and improve accountability, while tenant farmers raise concerns over access to subsidised inputs

title: Trinamool Congress leader and Falta candidate Jahangir Khan arrested near Nepal border
content: Jahangir Khan had withdrawn from the contest at Falta re-poll on May 21 and has been on the run since the polling was held

title: Revanth Reddy charges Kishan Reddy with influencing Union Ministers against granting approvals to Telangana projects
content: The Chief Minister challenges BJP and BRS for a debate on developmental projects in the State since last 12 years

title: What Forced Nearly 100 Schools To Shut In Japan? A Bear
content: A bear roaming the streets of a Japanese city for three days forced authorities to shut nearly 100 schools on Monday as hunters, police and local officials launched a major search operation.

title: Disbanded Tripura Groups Announce 72-Hour Blockade Over Pending Demands
content: The agreement included provisions for economic assistance, livelihood opportunities and other welfare measures aimed at facilitating their long-term rehabilitation.

title: UP Cops Prevent Suicide After Meta Alert Over Instagram Post
content: A 25-year-old man's life was saved after Meerut Police acted on a suicide alert received from Meta following an Instagram post, reaching him within eight minutes and getting him admitted to a hospital, officials said.

title: Naga Villager Killed In Attack By Suspected Insurgents In Manipur
content: Manipur Chief Minister Y Khemchand Singh condemned the killing of the civilian, and said the security forces are carrying out operations to bring the attackers to justice

title: INDIA Bloc Buries Hatchet, Recalibrates, Announces 5-Point Plan
content: Amid the shifting dynamics that saw the Congress come up on top with Kerala under its belt and a driving seat in Tamil Nadu, the overriding message of unity came from the Grand Old Party.

title: After Years Of Naxal Warfare, Elite CoBRA Commandos' New Challenge: Manipur
content: The Commando Battalion for Resolute Action (CoBRA) is the CRPF's specialised jungle warfare unit and has played a major role in anti-Naxal operations across central India.

title: 100 Days Of War, 60 Days Of Fragile Peace. It All Fell Apart In 24 Hours
content: As Israel and Iran traded blows, the wider regional fallout was immediate. Iraq's Civil Aviation Authority announced a 72-hour closure of Iraqi airspace in response to the renewed attacks.

title: US Iran War Live: Israel, Iran Announce Halting Of Strikes As Trump Demands A 'Stop'
content: US Iran War LIVE Updates: Iran closed the airspace around Tehran's Imam Khomeini International Airport, the country's main airfield, after the Israeli attack.

title: They Stole Wallet Loaded With Cash On A Bus. Then Luck Ran Out
content: The accused, identified as Radha, a native of Karnataka, and her male companion Ratheesh, were later arrested from their rented accommodation in Kerala's Ernakulam district.

title: Punjab's 'Protest Politics' Comes Full Circle For Arvind Kejriwal And AAP
content: What makes these protests politically significant is that many of these groups were among AAP's most enthusiastic supporters before the 2022 elections

title: "India Stands In Solidarity": PM Modi On Philippines Earthquake That Killed 32
content: An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines on Monday, killing at least 32 people, injuring more than 200 others mostly in damaged buildings and sending a 1-metre (3-foot) tsunami into nearby coasts.

title: 18-Year-Old Woman Kidnapped In Assam Found Inside Sack At Railway Station
content: A massive search operation has been launched by Dibrugarh Police to identify and arrest those responsible.

title: Trinamool Split, Cracks In INDIA Bloc: Hardeep Puri Says Next Target Punjab
content: Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri, speaking to NDTV's Senior Executive Editor Aditya Raj Kaul as the INDIA bloc held a meeting in New Delhi, was unsparing in his assessment of the Opposition's condition.

title: 'India Won't Pay Toll To Pass Through Strait Of Hormuz': Hardeep Puri To NDTV
content: "There is some arrangement at play with Iran. They have been good to us. I mean, all sides have been good to us," Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas Hardeep Singh Puri said

title: Probe Panel Formed 2 Days After Late-Night Roadside Showdown In Chhattisgarh
content: A young man travelling with his wife and child was allegedly assaulted during a late-night police checking drive in Chhattisgarh's Dhamtari.

title: NASA Goes Luxe With Prada Spacesuits For Artemis 4 Moon Landing
content: The new product follows Prada's splashy foray into space fashion in 2024 with the unveiling of a spacesuit that is expected to be used for NASA's anticipated Artemis 4 moon landing in 2028.

title: Will Indians Be Shielded From Fuel Price Shock? What Hardeep Puri told NDTV
content: The Middle East crisis has complicated India's energy arithmetic. Roughly 90% of India's crude imports and nearly 60 per cent of its LPG imports passed through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Civil Society Groups Hold Rally To Demand Postponement Of Manipur Census
content: Hundreds of participants carrying placards and banners joined the procession, raising slogans against the Census exercise and urging the authorities to defer it until normalcy is restored in the state.

title: SWAMIH Fund explained: How it works, eligibility, and what's next
content: The TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of The Times of India. With a mission to provide valuable insights and updates, the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce and can navigate the complexities of the business world. title: Indian-American surges into LA Mayoral runoff spot, tiggers Trump tirade
content: the slow-moving vote count has reignited one of president Donald Trump's favourite political themes . he wrote, "No way this could have happened. Rigged Election!" in a post on Truth Social, he said: "two great Republican Candidates are being cheated"
